attacking_style: Attacks from reduced groups on climbs 14-23km from finish, targeting final or penultimate ascents. Struggles with sprint timing on uphill finishes, often going 100-300m too early.
team_tactics: Receives full team support for uphill sprint finishes but leadout execution inconsistent. Sometimes deployed as tactical passenger in breaks when teammates are behind.
terrain_preference: Excels on extended climbs in stage races and classics (Giro stages, Flanders hills). Vulnerable on downhill accelerations and punchy uphill sprint finishes requiring precise timing.
vulnerability: Uphill sprint timing consistently problematic - goes too early, leading out rivals (3+ instances). Cannot respond to explosive downhill attacks. Crashes have ended promising races.
key_patterns: Misjudges uphill sprint timing by 100-300m, accelerating early and fading (3 documented instances)
Attacks decisively from breakaways on climbs 14-23km out, not waiting for final kilometer
Shows unexpected climbing form for classics specialist, attacking on category climbs in Grand Tours
Displays exceptional late-season/Tour prep form with sprint wins against pure sprinters (Poland, Suisse)
As defending champion, unable to respond to explosive attacks (Strade Bianche, Flanders 2020)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed both crash (race management issue at Strade Bianche) and continued sprint timing problems at Giro, suggesting underlying tactical issues persist despite prior form peaks
